title: Intensely Garlicky Korean Baby Lettuce Bokkeum
description: A garlicky and crunchy Korean dish featuring baby lettuce leaves, perfect for a midnight craving. This kosher-friendly recipe is made in the traditional Gangwon style.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, and sesame oil.
Add the minced garlic to the bowl and whisk until well combined.
Add the dehydrated baby lettuce leaves to the skillet and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Pour the garlic-soy sauce mixture over the lettuce leaves and stir-fry for an additional 2 minutes.
Season with salt and crushed black pepper to taste.
Lay a corn husk flat and place a portion of the lettuce mixture in the center.
Fold the corn husk over the filling to form a tight wrap, and repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Serve the bokkeum hot, wrapped in the corn husks.
